rationale: In the theory development section, this citation is used not only as broad prior work on online content sharing but also to support the paper's definition of elaboration and the linguistic indicators used to capture it. The citing paper then operationalizes elaboration with text features such as word count and words per sentence in its own analyses. Although cited alongside other papers, the role goes beyond a passing mention because it informs the conceptual setup and measurement approach.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Melumad et al. (2019)'s text-based conceptualization of user-generated content characteristics, especially elaboration-related linguistic features, to an online learning context. It uses that prior work to justify and implement its mechanism analysis of idea versus identity sharing in learner posts.
